title: Scheduled Quality Sweeps
one_liner: Not every check fits in a commit. Some run on a cadence — and produce the same evidence shape as inline gates.
problem: Whole-system audits — drift against the spec (P-09), dependency-vulnerability scans, architecture conformance, dead-code surveys — never fire in a per-commit pipeline. Done manually, they go stale within weeks; done as one-off scripts, their reports drift away from the trust-layer format and nobody trusts them. Without a regular cadence, the system rots in the gaps between gates.
solution: Run named sweeps on a schedule or on a global trigger ("nightly drift check", "weekly security sweep", "monthly architecture review"). Each sweep is the same kind of object as a gate (P-32): a role, a standard, a structured verdict with evidence and follow-up tasks. Reports land in the trust-layer queue alongside per-commit findings, so reviewers see one stream of evidence — not one stream per script. Sweeps inform the spec-drift loop (P-09) and the pipeline definition (P-31), closing the meta-loop.
